Module Contents

utils.scale_samples(samples, lower_bounds, upper_bounds)[source]

Scale each column of samples from [0, 1] to [lb_j, ub_j].

Parameters:

  • samples (ndarray) – A (d, n) array of samples in [0, 1]^n.

  • lower_bounds (array-like) – Length-n array of lower bounds.

  • upper_bounds (array-like) – Length-n array of upper bounds.

Returns:

A (d, n) array with each column scaled to its corresponding bounds.

Return type:

ndarray

utils.flatten_der_indices(indices)[source]
utils.nrmse(y_true, y_pred, norm_type='minmax')[source]

Compute the Normalized Root Mean Squared Error (NRMSE).

Parameters:

  • y_true (array-like) – Ground truth values.

  • y_pred (array-like) – Predicted values.

  • norm_type (str) – Normalization type: - ‘minmax’: divide by (max - min) of y_true - ‘mean’: divide by mean of y_true - ‘std’: divide by standard deviation of y_true

Returns:

NRMSE value.

Return type:

float

utils.transform_nested_list(nested_list)[source]

Recursively traverse a nested list. Whenever we encounter a two-element list [k, v] with both k and v as integers, transform k -> 2*k.

utils.convert_index_to_exponent_form(lst)[source]
utils.build_companion_array(nvars, order, der_indices)[source]
utils.compare_OTI_indices(nvars, order, term_check)[source]

Generate list of lists that contain indices of bases in an OTI number

Generate list of lists that contain indices of bases in an OTI number
